sentences.sentence: If you go back far enough, everything lived in the sea.
sentences.translation: 如果追溯得足够久远，一切生物都曾生活在海里。

structure.text: If you go back far enough

difficult_points: If 从句表示假设的追溯条件，go back 指追溯到过去。
everything 作单数主语，lived in the sea 表示曾经的状态。

sentences.sentence: At various points in evolutionary history, enterprising individuals within many different animal groups moved out onto the land, sometimes even to the most parched deserts, taking their own private seawater with them in blood and cellular fluids.
sentences.translation: 在进化史的不同阶段，许多动物群体中的先行者走上陆地，有时甚至进入最干旱的沙漠，并把自己的“海水”带在血液和细胞液中。

structure.text: At various points in evolutionary history
enterprising individuals within many different animal groups
sometimes even to the most parched deserts
taking their own private seawater with them in blood and cellular fluids

difficult_points: taking... 是分词伴随结构，说明登陆时携带的东西。
private seawater 是比喻用法，具体指血液和细胞液中的水分。

sentences.sentence: In addition to the reptiles, birds, mammals and insects which we see all around us, other groups that have succeeded out of water include scorpions, snails, crustaceans such as woodlice and land crabs, millipedes and centipedes, spiders and various worms.
sentences.translation: 除了我们身边常见的爬行动物、鸟类、哺乳动物和昆虫外，成功离开水域的其他群体还包括蝎子、蜗牛、木虱和陆蟹等甲壳动物、千足虫、蜈蚣、蜘蛛以及各种蠕虫。

structure.text: In addition to the reptiles, birds, mammals and insects which we see all around us
which we see all around us
other groups that have succeeded out of water
that have succeeded out of water
scorpions, snails, crustaceans such as woodlice and land crabs, millipedes and centipedes, spiders and various worms

difficult_points: 两个 which/that 从句分别修饰 insects 和 groups。
succeeded out of water 意为成功离开水域，such as 后列举甲壳动物例子。

sentences.sentence: And we mustn't forget the plants, without whose prior invasion of the land none of the other migrations could have happened.
sentences.translation: 我们也不能忘记植物；如果没有植物先行占领陆地，其他迁移都不可能发生。

structure.text: without whose prior invasion of the land none of the other migrations could have happened
without whose prior invasion of the land
none of the other migrations could have happened

difficult_points: without whose 引导带关系代词的介词结构，whose 指 plants。
could have happened 是对过去事实的虚拟推断，表示“本不可能发生”。

sentences.sentence: Moving from water to land involved a major redesign of every aspect of life, including breathing and reproduction.
sentences.translation: 从水域迁移到陆地，意味着生命各个方面都要进行重大改造，包括呼吸和繁殖。

structure.text: a major redesign of every aspect of life

difficult_points: Moving from water to land 是动名词短语作主语。
including 后列出 redesign 涉及的具体方面。

sentences.sentence: Nevertheless, a good number of thoroughgoing land animals later turned around, abandoned their hard-earned terrestrial re-tooling, and returned to the water again.
sentences.translation: 然而，相当多彻底适应陆地的动物后来改变方向，放弃来之不易的陆地适应，再次回到水中。

structure.text: a good number of thoroughgoing land animals
later turned around, abandoned their hard-earned terrestrial re-tooling, and returned to the water again

difficult_points: 三个过去时动词并列，依次描述动物重新入水的过程。
hard-earned 修饰 terrestrial re-tooling，强调陆地适应来之不易。

sentences.sentence: Seals have only gone part way back.
sentences.translation: 海豹只回到了水中生活的一部分程度。
difficult_points: 现在完成时 have gone 表示回归水中的过程已经发生。
part way back 表示“只走了一部分回头路”，暗示仍保留陆地特征。

sentences.sentence: They show us what the intermediates might have been like, on the way to extreme cases such as whales and dugongs.
sentences.translation: 它们让我们看到，走向鲸鱼和儒艮等极端类型的过程中间形态可能是什么样。

structure.text: what the intermediates might have been like
on the way to extreme cases such as whales and dugongs

difficult_points: what...might have been like 是宾语从句，表示“可能是什么样”。
might have been 是对过去中间形态的推测。

sentences.sentence: Whales (including the small whales we call dolphins) and dugongs, with their close cousins the manatees, ceased to be land creatures altogether and reverted to the full marine habits of their remote ancestors.
sentences.translation: 鲸类（包括我们称为海豚的小型鲸）和儒艮，以及它们的近亲海牛，彻底不再是陆地生物，恢复了远古祖先的完整海洋习性。

structure.text: Whales (including the small whales we call dolphins) and dugongs
with their close cousins the manatees
ceased to be land creatures altogether and reverted to the full marine habits of their remote ancestors

difficult_points: we call dolphins 是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰 small whales。
ceased to be 与 reverted to 并列，描述彻底回归海洋。

sentences.sentence: They don't even come ashore to breed.
sentences.translation: 它们甚至不上岸繁殖。
difficult_points: to breed 是目的不定式，说明上岸的目的。
don't even 强调连最基本的上岸繁殖都不进行。

sentences.sentence: They do, however, still breathe air, having never developed anything equivalent to the gills of their earlier marine incarnation.
sentences.translation: 不过，它们仍然呼吸空气，因为从未进化出相当于早期海洋形态鳃的器官。

structure.text: having never developed anything equivalent to the gills of their earlier marine incarnation

difficult_points: do breathe 用 do 强调“确实呼吸空气”。
having never developed 是完成时分词，说明从未进化出鳃这一背景。

sentences.sentence: Turtles went back to the sea a very long time ago and, like all vertebrate returnees to the water, they breathe air.
sentences.translation: 海龟很久以前回到海中，而且像所有重新入水的脊椎动物一样，它们呼吸空气。

structure.text: went back to the sea a very long time ago
like all vertebrate returnees to the water

difficult_points: like 短语作比较状语，returnees 指重新回到水中的动物。
and 连接两个分句，第二个 they 指 turtles。

sentences.sentence: However, they are, in one respect, less fully given back to the water than whales or dugongs, for turtles still lay their eggs on beaches.
sentences.translation: 不过，在一个方面，它们回归水中的程度不如鲸或儒艮彻底，因为海龟仍在海滩上产卵。

structure.text: are, in one respect, less fully given back to the water than whales or dugongs
for turtles still lay their eggs on beaches

difficult_points: are given back to the water 是被动表达，比较对象是 whales or dugongs。
for 引导原因分句，说明海龟回归不彻底的依据。

sentences.sentence: There is evidence that all modern turtles are descended from a terrestrial ancestor which lived before most of the dinosaurs.
sentences.translation: 有证据表明，所有现代海龟都源自一种生活在大多数恐龙出现之前的陆地祖先。

structure.text: evidence that all modern turtles are descended from a terrestrial ancestor
which lived before most of the dinosaurs

difficult_points: There is evidence that... 中 that 引导 evidence 的内容。
be descended from 表示“源自”；which 修饰 ancestor。

sentences.sentence: There are two key fossils called Proganochelys quenstedti and Palaeochersis talampayensis dating from early dinosaur times, which appear to be close to the ancestry of all modern turtles and tortoises.
sentences.translation: 有两块关键化石，名为Proganochelys quenstedti和Palaeochersis talampayensis，年代可追溯到恐龙早期，似乎接近所有现代海龟和陆龟的祖先。

structure.text: two key fossils called Proganochelys quenstedti and Palaeochersis talampayensis
called Proganochelys quenstedti and Palaeochersis talampayensis
which appear to be close to the ancestry of all modern turtles and tortoises

difficult_points: called 与 dating from 两个分词短语后置修饰 fossils。
which 从句说明这些化石与现代龟类祖先的关系。

expressions.text: be close to the ancestry of

sentences.sentence: You might wonder how we can tell whether fossil animals lived on land or in water, especially if only fragments are found.
sentences.translation: 你可能会想知道，尤其只发现碎片时，我们如何判断化石动物生活在陆地还是水中。

structure.text: how we can tell whether fossil animals lived on land or in water
whether fossil animals lived on land or in water
especially if only fragments are found

difficult_points: how 引导 wonder 的宾语从句，内含 whether...or... 选择结构。
if 从句使用被动语态，only fragments 是 found 的主语。

sentences.sentence: Sometimes it's obvious.
sentences.translation: 有时答案很明显。
difficult_points: it 指判断化石生活环境这件事，属于指代性主语。
obvious 表示判断结果清楚，不是“可见的物体”。

sentences.sentence: Ichthyosaurs were reptilian contemporaries of the dinosaurs, with fins and streamlined bodies.
sentences.translation: 鱼龙是恐龙时代的爬行动物，同时拥有鳍和流线型身体。
difficult_points: contemporaries of the dinosaurs 表示与恐龙同时代的生物。
with 短语补充描述身体特征。

sentences.sentence: The fossils look like dolphins, and they surely lived like dolphins, in the water.
sentences.translation: 这些化石看起来像海豚，而且它们肯定也像海豚一样生活在水中。

structure.text: and they surely lived like dolphins

difficult_points: look like 与 lived like 分别表示外形相似和生活方式相似。
and 连接两个独立分句，they 指 fossils 所代表的动物。

sentences.sentence: With turtles it is a little less obvious.
sentences.translation: 对于海龟来说，情况就不那么明显了。
difficult_points: With turtles 表示“就海龟而言”，不是伴随关系。
it 是形式主语，指判断其生活环境这件事。

sentences.sentence: One way to tell is by measuring the bones of their forelimbs.
sentences.translation: 一种判断方法是测量它们前肢的骨骼。

structure.text: by measuring the bones of their forelimbs

difficult_points: One way to tell 中 to tell 是不定式后置修饰 way。
by measuring 说明判断方法，their 指 turtles。

sentences.sentence: Walter Joyce and Jacques Gauthier, at Yale University, obtained three measurements in these particular bones of 71 species of living turtles and tortoises.
sentences.translation: 耶鲁大学的沃尔特·乔伊斯和雅克·高蒂尔，对71种现生海龟和陆龟的这些特定骨骼进行了三项测量。

structure.text: three measurements in these particular bones of 71 species of living turtles and tortoises

difficult_points: at Yale University 是插入地点信息，不改变主干。
of 71 species 修饰 living turtles and tortoises，说明样本范围。

sentences.sentence: They used a kind of triangular graph paper to plot the three measurements against one another.
sentences.translation: 他们使用一种三角形坐标纸，将三项测量值相互绘图比较。

structure.text: a kind of triangular graph paper
to plot the three measurements against one another

difficult_points: to plot 表示使用坐标纸的目的。
against one another 表示彼此对照比较。

sentences.sentence: All the land tortoise species formed a tight cluster of points in the upper part of the triangle; all the water turtles cluster in the lower part of the triangular graph.
sentences.translation: 所有陆龟物种在三角形上部形成紧密的点群；所有水龟则聚集在三角图的下部。

structure.text: a tight cluster of points in the upper part of the triangle
cluster in the lower part of the triangular graph

difficult_points: 分号连接两个并列观察结果，第二句用一般现在时作概括。
cluster 作动词表示聚集成团，upper/lower part 对应上下位置。

sentences.sentence: There was no overlap, except when they added some species that spend time both in water and on land.
sentences.translation: 两组没有重叠，除非把一些既生活在水中又生活在陆地上的物种加入进来。

structure.text: except when they added some species
that spend time both in water and on land

difficult_points: There was 结构的真正主语是 no overlap。
that 从句修饰 species，both...and... 表示水陆兼有。

sentences.sentence: Sure enough, these amphibious species show up on the triangular graph approximately halfway between the 'wet cluster' of sea tortoises and the 'dry cluster' of land tortoises.
sentences.translation: 果然，这些两栖物种出现在三角图上，位置大致处于海龟“湿群”和陆龟“干群”的中间。

structure.text: approximately halfway between the 'wet cluster' of sea tortoises and the 'dry cluster' of land tortoises

difficult_points: show up 表示“出现”；Sure enough 表示结果与预期相符。
halfway between...and... 表示位于两群之间的大致中点。

sentences.sentence: The next step was to determine where the fossils fell.
sentences.translation: 下一步是确定这些化石落在图上的什么位置。

structure.text: to determine where the fossils fell

difficult_points: to determine 是表语不定式，说明下一步任务。
where the fossils fell 中 fell 指在图上的落点，不是跌倒。

sentences.sentence: The bones of P. quenstedti and P. talampayensis leave us in no doubt.
sentences.translation: P. quenstedti和P. talampayensis的骨骼让我们毫无疑问。

structure.text: The bones of P. quenstedti and P. talampayensis

difficult_points: leave someone in no doubt 是固定表达，表示“使某人毫无疑问”。
of 连接化石名称与 bones，说明所属关系。

sentences.sentence: Their points on the graph are right in the thick of the dry cluster.
sentences.translation: 它们在图上的点恰好位于“干群”的核心位置。

structure.text: right in the thick of the dry cluster

difficult_points: Their 指两种化石；points 是它们在图上的数据点。
in the thick of 表示“正处于核心/密集之中”。

sentences.sentence: Both these fossils were dry-land tortoises.
sentences.translation: 这两种化石都属于陆地陆龟。
difficult_points: Both 作限定词表示“两者都”，谓语用复数 were。
dry-land 强调生活在陆地上，与水龟相对。

sentences.sentence: They come from the era before our turtles returned to the water.
sentences.translation: 它们来自海龟重新回到水中之前的时代。

structure.text: the era before our turtles returned to the water
before our turtles returned to the water

difficult_points: They 指两种化石；come from 表示年代来源。
before 从句说明 era 的时间位置。

sentences.sentence: You might think, therefore, that modern land tortoises have probably stayed on land ever since those early terrestrial times, as most mammals did after a few of them went back to the sea.
sentences.translation: 因此，你可能会认为现代陆龟自早期陆地时代以来一直生活在陆地上，就像大多数哺乳动物在少数成员回到海中后所做的那样。

structure.text: that modern land tortoises have probably stayed on land ever since those early terrestrial times
as most mammals did after a few of them went back to the sea
after a few of them went back to the sea

difficult_points: have stayed ever since 表示从过去一直持续到现在。
as most mammals did 中 did 代替 stayed on land，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: But apparently not.
sentences.translation: 但显然并非如此。
difficult_points: not 省略了前句的 have stayed on land，构成简略否定回答。
apparently 表示根据证据得出的“显然”。

sentences.sentence: If you draw out the family tree of all modern turtles and tortoises, nearly all the branches are aquatic.
sentences.translation: 如果画出现代所有海龟和陆龟的家谱，几乎所有分支都是水生的。

structure.text: If you draw out the family tree of all modern turtles and tortoises

difficult_points: If 从句提出观察方法，draw out 表示“画出、展开”。
aquatic 作表语表示水生的。

sentences.sentence: Today's land tortoises constitute a single branch, deeply nested among branches consisting of aquatic turtles.
sentences.translation: 如今的陆龟构成单独一个分支，深深嵌在由水龟组成的分支之中。

structure.text: deeply nested among branches consisting of aquatic turtles

difficult_points: deeply nested 是过去分词短语，补充说明该分支在谱系中的位置。
consisting of 修饰 branches，表示这些分支由水龟组成。

sentences.sentence: This suggests that modern land tortoises have not stayed on land continuously since the time of P. quenstedti and P. talampayensis.
sentences.translation: 这说明现代陆龟并不是自P. quenstedti和P. talampayensis所处时代以来一直生活在陆地上。

structure.text: that modern land tortoises have not stayed on land continuously since the time of P. quenstedti and P. talampayensis

difficult_points: suggests 后接 that 宾语从句；have not stayed since 表示对持续性的否定。
This 指前句陆龟分支嵌在水龟分支中的谱系证据。

sentences.sentence: Rather, their ancestors were among those who went back to the water, and they then re-emerged back onto the land in (relatively) more recent times.
sentences.translation: 相反，它们的祖先属于那些回到水中的动物，后来又在相对较近的时期重新登上陆地。

structure.text: were among those who went back to the water
who went back to the water
and they then re-emerged back onto the land in (relatively) more recent times

difficult_points: who 从句修饰 those，说明这些祖先曾回到水中。
re-emerged 表示再次出现/重新登陆，back 强调回到陆地。

sentences.sentence: Tortoises therefore represent a remarkable double return.
sentences.translation: 因此，陆龟代表了一次非凡的“双重回归”。
difficult_points: therefore 表示由前文证据得出的结论。
double return 指先从海到陆、再从陆到水并最终回陆的多次迁移。

sentences.sentence: In common with all mammals, reptiles and birds, their remote ancestors were marine fish and before that various more or less worm-like creatures stretching back, still in the sea, to the primeval bacteria.
sentences.translation: 和所有哺乳动物、爬行动物及鸟类一样，陆龟的远古祖先是海鱼；再往前则是各种或多或少像蠕虫的生物，一直在海中追溯到原始细菌。

structure.text: In common with all mammals, reptiles and birds
and before that various more or less worm-like creatures stretching back, still in the sea, to the primeval bacteria
stretching back, still in the sea, to the primeval bacteria

difficult_points: In common with 表示“和……一样”，比较对象是其他脊椎动物。
stretching back... 是分词短语，说明祖先谱系可追溯到原始细菌。

sentences.sentence: Later ancestors lived on land and stayed there for a very large number of generations.
sentences.translation: 后来的祖先生活在陆地上，并在那里延续了许多代。

structure.text: lived on land and stayed there
for a very large number of generations

difficult_points: lived 与 stayed 并列，there 指 land。
for 引出持续时间，generations 表示世代。

sentences.sentence: Later ancestors still evolved back into the water and became sea turtles.
sentences.translation: 再后来的祖先又进化回水中，成为海龟。

structure.text: evolved back into the water and became

difficult_points: evolved back into 与 became 并列，描述回到水中的进化结果。
still 表示时间顺序上的“再后来”。

sentences.sentence: And finally they returned yet again to the land as tortoises, some of which now live in the driest of deserts.
sentences.translation: 最后，它们又一次以陆龟的形态回到陆地，其中一些如今生活在最干旱的沙漠里。

structure.text: returned yet again to the land
some of which now live in the driest of deserts

difficult_points: yet again 强调这是再次回归；as tortoises 表示回归时的形态。
some of which 指 tortoises，which 引导非限制性定语从句。
